verbGlossary.SCOPED BY: {source} scope or warranty coverage is defined in terms of {target} divisions (e.g., subcontract scope of work scoped by specification sections, warranty scoped to Division 07 roofing specification)
verbGlossary.ORGANIZED BY: {source} or scope document is structured according to {target} divisions (e.g., bid package broken into MasterFormat divisions for trade separation)
verbGlossary.CODED BY: Line items in a procurement or contract document are assigned classification codes from {target} (e.g., purchase order line items coded by standard cost codes)
verbGlossary.INCORPORATES: {source} instrument formally includes {target} requirements by reference (e.g., subcontract incorporates specification sections as contract exhibits)
verbGlossary.BILLED AGAINST: {source} payment applications and invoices reference {target} breakdown structure (e.g., subcontractor progress billing against WBS codes)
verbGlossary.ALLOCATED AGAINST: {source}cost on a purchase order or contract is mapped to {target} structure for cost tracking (e.g., material PO allocated against budget codes)
verbGlossary.GOVERNED BY: {source} instrument or term is regulated by {target} published form or code (e.g., prime contract governed by AIA A201 general conditions, general conditions governed by IBC fire separation requirements)
verbGlossary.STRUCTURED PER: {source} or procurement approach follows {target} prescribed delivery framework (e.g., prime contract structured per design-build or CM-at-risk delivery method classification)
verbGlossary.COMPLIES WITH: {source} document or insurance certificate confirms adherence to {target} or standard (e.g., general conditions comply with IBC accessibility requirements, closeout package complies with LEED documentation requirements)
verbGlossary.BENCHMARKED TO: {source} metric or term is compared against {target} published industry norms (e.g., retainage percentage benchmarked to AACE recommended practices, bid evaluation benchmarked to AACE cost estimate classifications)
verbGlossary.ENFORCED PER: {source} penalty or remedy clause is administered following {target} prescribed procedures (e.g., liquidated damages enforced per AIA contract document provisions)
verbGlossary.VERIFIED AGAINST: {source} is validated against {target} compliance requirements (e.g., subcontractor qualification verified against OSHA recordkeeping history, closeout verified against energy code commissioning requirements)
verbGlossary.SCREENED AGAINST: {source}is evaluated against {target} safety or hazard criteria during prequalification (e.g., contractor safety record screened against OSHA hazard categories and EMR thresholds)
verbGlossary.TAGGED TO: {source} scope item is linked to {target} for element-level traceability (e.g., warranty item tagged to a UniFormat building element for tracking coverage by system)
verbGlossary.TRACKED BY: {source} allowance or contingency is monitored over time using {target} breakdown structure (e.g., allowance drawdowns tracked by WBS code)
verbGlossary.CLASSIFIED UNDER: {source}document is categorized using {target} taxonomy (e.g., prevailing wage certification classified under standard cost codes for labor rate tracking)
verbGlossary.SEGMENTED BY: {source} or procurement metric is broken down by {target} for comparative analysis (e.g., change order rate segmented by WBS code to identify problem trades, DBE participation segmented by MasterFormat division)
